calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Neal McGettigan for and with (executor) John Murray.

The Court asked if there were any additional arguments. Mr. McGettigan stated that he had nothing to add. Mr. Murray addressed the extraordinary fees, that there was property in Pacific Grove in disrepair and tenants who were not paying rent, that the Otter Street property also had tenant issues but they finally were able to get her out, that a neighbor of the Pacific Grove property complained about a tree causing damage, they hired an arborist to check it who confirmed it needed to come down, that they obtained a permit to remove it but the local people protested, they had to appear before a committee who could not decide the case, it was continued for a month and then came before the committee which now had some new members, that they finally got the okay to remove the tree and obtained a new permit, it was appealed and they had to hire an attorney and that it was finally removed but not without incurring a lot of expenses. Mr. McGettigan stated that in regards to the time share interest it is a headache to whoever receives it. Mr. Murray stated that the time share was to go to his wife, that she does not want it, that the manager in Tahiti absconded with funds and that it is no longer of any value. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Final Inventory And Appraisal.
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Off Calendar

Petition For Preliminary Distribution.
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jane Murray for and with John Murray (Executor). Steven Weinkauf for the Public Administrators Office. Also present, Neal McGettigan.

Ms. Murray provided an endorsed copy of a declaration that was filed this morning. The court once again expressed concern with regard to bequests to family members, competency in Probate law, duty of independent judgment and appearance of violation of rules. Mr. Murray explained the history and relationship with the decedent. The court raised the issue of independent representation. Mr. Murray stated that they are prepared to have Ms. Murray substitute out and have attorney Neal McGettigan step in, explained that the only thing left to be done is to sell the properties and that they are prepared to close the estate in September. Mr. McGettigan agrees to substitute in. The court indicated that preliminary will be authorized but that fees will not. The petition for preliminary distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution.
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Continued To Jul-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jane Murray for and with John Murray.

The Parties addressed the Court's concern regarding the issue of conflict with Jane Murray (Daughter) representing John Murray (Father) in this case, Mr. Murray preparing the original will and the amended will, whether Ms. Murray has authority to appear in a civil matter, other family members/children being named as beneficiaries, whether there is a fee contract, etc. Ms. Murray explained what services she has provided/performed and what services her Father has performed, and stated that Chief Deputy Alexander McMahon was aware of her involvement. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution was continued to July 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed the Clerk to contact Counsel for the Public Administrator to request that he appear at the next hearing. Ms. Murray is to file a declaration from Mr. McMahon indicating that she has authority to represent her Father in this case.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution.
Off Calendar Jun-18-2014 Continued To Jul-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Preliminary Distribution.
Off Calendar May-28-2014 Continued To Jun-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Murray in Propria Persona.

Mr. Murray stated that all three individuals (Eric, Marie and Bernadette) all live together, that the mailing to the Disabled Veterans did not get returned, and that the Marin Mammal Center/Marine Mammal Center is one entity and nothing has come back from them. Mr. Murray also stated that Mr. Antonelli received the gift of the automobile ten years ago by Ms. Holland and that no one knows of his where abouts. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Warren Wong is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
